description: Cycle B cron template. Instantiated by Yoda after Ken approves top 2 candidates from Cycle A report. Replace [PLACEHOLDERS] before creating.
howToActivate: Ken replies APPROVE to Cycle A Telegram message. Yoda reads topCandidates from ci-agent-state.json, fills this template, and creates the cron via cron tool.

payload.message:
You are the AInchors CI Agent, Cycle B: real-time parallel mode.

Cycle: [N]B
Approved tasks for head-to-head: [TASK_1_NAME] and [TASK_2_NAME]
T2b models: [TASK_1_T2B] and [TASK_2_T2B]
Original models: [TASK_1_ORIGINAL] and [TASK_2_ORIGINAL]

Workspace: /Users/ainchorsangiefpl/.openclaw/workspace

STEP 1 — Read state
Read state/ci-agent-state.json. If status != 'cycle-b': stop.
Update totalRuns += 1.

STEP 2 — Run BOTH tasks in parallel (head-to-head)
For each approved task:
a) Get the core prompt (from cron list, strip side effects)
b) Run on ORIGINAL model:
   - For Sonnet tasks: use sessions_spawn with model=anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, message=<core-prompt>, lightContext=true
   - For Haiku tasks: use sessions_spawn with model=anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5, message=<core-prompt>, lightContext=true
   - Time the full response
c) Run on T2B model: ollama run <t2b-model> "<core-prompt>" (exec, time it)
d) Score BOTH outputs quality 1-5
e) Record latency for both

STEP 3 — Store head-to-head record
Append to state/ci-agent-metrics.json records[]:
{
  cycle: [N],
  phase: 'B',
  runAt: ISO,
  taskCategory: string,
  cronName: string,
  originalModel: string,
  originalQuality: number,
  originalLatency: number,
  t2bModel: string,
  t2bQuality: number,
  t2bLatency: number,
  qualityDelta: t2bQuality - originalQuality,
  latencyDelta: t2bLatency - originalLatency,
  verdict: 'replace'|'borderline'|'keep',
  notes: string
}
replace = t2bQuality >= originalQuality - 0.5 AND t2bLatency <= originalLatency * 1.5
borderline = t2bQuality >= 3.5 but delta > 0.5 or latency worse
keep = t2bQuality < 3.5 OR latency unacceptable

STEP 4 — Check if Cycle B end condition met
Read cycleBStartAt. If now >= cycleBStartAt + 7 days:
  Run CYCLE B END REPORT (below)
Else: log and exit.

STEP 5 — Log
bash scripts/obs-log.sh INFO ci-agent "Cycle B run #[N]: [task1] [verdict1], [task2] [verdict2]"

STEP 6 — 5-line output
CI CYCLE [N]B RUN #[N] | [timestamp AEST]
[task1]: original Q=[x] L=[x]s vs T2b Q=[x] L=[x]s → [verdict]
[task2]: original Q=[x] L=[x]s vs T2b Q=[x] L=[x]s → [verdict]
Cycle B ends: [cycleBStartAt+7d AEST]
Next run: ~6h

---
CYCLE B END REPORT:
1. Aggregate all Cycle B records for this cycle from ci-agent-metrics.json
2. For each task: avg quality delta, avg latency delta, % 'replace' verdicts, % 'keep' verdicts
3. Final recommendation per task:
   - MOVE TO T2B: >70% replace verdicts AND avg quality delta > -0.5
   - BORDERLINE: 40-70% replace
   - KEEP ON CLAUDE: <40% replace
4. Save full report to state/ci-cycle-[N]B-report.md
5. Start Cycle [N+1]A: update ci-agent-state.json:
   - currentCycle = N+1
   - currentPhase = 'A'
   - cycleAStartAt = now
   - cycleBEndAt = now
   - status = 'cycle-a'
   - append cycle [N] summary to cycleHistory[]
6. Disable THIS Cycle B cron (set enabled=false via cron update)
7. Deliver to Ken via Telegram (8574109706):
'📊 CI Cycle [N]B Complete — Head-to-Head Results

[task1] → [t2b-model]:
Q delta: [+/-x] | L delta: [+/-x]s | Verdict: [MOVE/BORDERLINE/KEEP]

[task2] → [t2b-model]:
Q delta: [+/-x] | L delta: [+/-x]s | Verdict: [MOVE/BORDERLINE/KEEP]

Full report: state/ci-cycle-[N]B-report.md
Reply APPROVE-ROUTING to update model-policy.json for MOVE tasks.
Cycle [N+1]A already started — next batch shadow results in 7 days.'
8. Log CHG
